etymology.description: 很(很) (hěn, "very") intensifies a quality or state. 好(好) (hǎo, "good") indicates a positive condition.
etymology.interpretation: 很(很) (hěn, "very") is used to intensify the following adjective, marking a high degree of the quality it describes. 好(好) (hǎo, "good") directly refers to a positive or desirable state. Together, 很好 signifies something that is exceptionally positive or favorable, combining intensity with a good condition.

examples.chinese: 你的中文说得很好(你的中文說得很好)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ de zhōngwén shuō de hěn hǎo.
examples.english: Your Chinese is spoken very well.

examples.chinese: 这道菜尝起来很好(這道菜嘗起來很好)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dào cài cháng qǐlái hěn hǎo.

examples.chinese: 天气很好(天氣很好)，我们去公园吧(我們去公園吧)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānqì hěn hǎo, wǒmen qù gōngyuán ba.
examples.english: The weather is very good, let's go to the park.
